title: Debug a WASM trap or panic
description: Turn an opaque trap into a source-level diagnosis using panic hooks, symbols, and boundary inspection.
body: Steps: 1. Reproduce the failure and capture the exact trap (unreachable, out-of-bounds memory access, integer divide) or panic message. 2. Ensure a panic hook is installed so Rust panics print a message and stack instead of a bare unreachable. 3. Build with debug info/DWARF and use the browser's WASM debugging (or a symbol map) to locate the source line. 4. Inspect the boundary call that preceded it for a bad pointer/length or a detached memory view. 5. Fix the root cause (bounds, ownership, target mismatch). Rollback point: diagnostic; read-only. Validation: the trap maps to a specific source location and the fix removes it under the reproducing input.
